title: paeonia officinalis subsp. italica
intro: Paeonia officinalis subsp. italica, also known as Italian peony, is a subspecies of the Paeonia officinalis plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Paeonia officinalis subsp. italica include relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
